programme.desc: Paid programming.

programme.title: Hidden Gold: Rockin' Riches
programme.desc: A record player sitting in storage turns out to be a potentially great discovery. This job is packed with all sorts of mismatched items: the gang also discovers an awesome wagon that dates back before the days of Flexible Flyer.

programme.title: Paid Program
programme.desc: Paid programming.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: Boarded Up Beauty
programme.desc: Nicole is on a mission to save vacant homes from the wrecking ball and her latest project is taking on a boarded up and previously condemned home from the late 80s.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: Kitchen Cabinets Remade
programme.desc: Turning her attention to the kitchen, Nicole must get creative and figure out how to update and reuse cabinets that where in the house.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: Back Yard on a Budget
programme.desc: Nicole doesn't have much to work with in the back yard, but she's making the best with what she's got. Lifting up the garage to pour a new foundation beats the cost of building a new one from scratch.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: Fire Damage Fixes
programme.desc: Nicole heads to her hometown of Detroit, Michigan to save a 1920s home that has been vacant since a fire in the house next door left it severely damaged.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: Exterior Miracles
programme.desc: Tired of the house being an eyesore in the neighborhood, Nicole must remove all the debris left over from demolishing the burnt out house on the lot next door.

programme.title: Rehab Addict: From Charred to Charming
programme.desc: In the home stretch on her project in Detroit, Nicole must finish up the upper apartment which is the area the fire damaged the most. The community comes together to build an urban garden.

programme.title: Boomtown Builder: 1912 Baker's House
programme.desc: A local sheriff and his wife call in Tim's help to modernize their home while keeping its historical nature. The original character of this house was stripped away by years of bad renovations, a bad design and a challenging layout.

programme.title: Boomtown Builder: 1915 Smelter House
programme.desc: Tim McClellan lends his talents to two amazing spaces that need reimagining in Clemenceau, Arizona. He creates wildly unique builds while unearthing the mysterious history of an adobe outbuilding.

programme.title: Boomtown Builder: 1914 Middle Management House
programme.desc: Tim helps an overwhelmed young family restore their house in Jerome, AZ. Originally built for a copper mine manager, Tim uncovers high-end details and adds even more authentic features to the once illustrious home.

programme.title: Boomtown Builder: 1912 Grocery Market House
programme.desc: Tim helps a couple in Jerome, AZ, tackle a 100-year-old converted grocery market. This monumental restoration creates special obstacles, including the removal of a load-bearing beam and custom-built furniture inspired by historical pieces.

programme.title: Boomtown Builder: 1931 Bootlegger's House
programme.desc: A family of seven calls on Tim McClellan to renovate their Cottonwood, AZ, home. He turns a storage area into a massive game room and builds a movable cocktail bar that reveals a 'secret' laundry room.

programme.title: Boomtown Builder: 1901 Printer's House
programme.desc: Tim helps a Jerome, Arizona, homeowner tackle an overwhelming restoration. He peels back decades of bad renovations to uncover clues that influence the direction of the build and solve the mystery of the home's original owner.

programme.title: Restored: 1901 Victorian Bungalow Restoration
programme.desc: Brett restores a Victorian Bungalow for a military veteran and his daughter. The project grows in scale when unexpected problems are discovered and it becomes a complete restoration.

programme.title: Restored: 1913 Craftsman Bungalow
programme.desc: Brett restores a 1913 craftsman bungalow for a young couple with a baby on the way. He's challenged with finding a way to bring back the natural wood in the house while still making it bright and welcoming.

programme.title: Restored: 1926 French Normandy Cottage
programme.desc: A cottage build in 1926 was a severely neglected rental for 30 years. Brett deals with a leaky roof, rotten windows, missing floorboards and damaged walls.

programme.title: Restored: 1928 Spanish Colonial Revival
programme.desc: Brett restores a classic Spanish Colonial Revival that hasn't been touched in years because the owner is a professional musician who is always on the road. He must make the kitchen and bath functional while staying true to the home's character.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: Meat in Music City
programme.desc: Roger Mooking heads to the country music capital Nashville, where chefs are playing meaty tunes. At Urban Grub, Chef Edgar Pendley fans the flames in a massive 18-foot-tall hearth and hangs a wall of house-made andouille sausages and pork ribs.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: Revolutionary Roasts
programme.desc: Mass. Roger puts the pedal to the metal on Chef Jeremy Stanton's 'Rotisserie Bike.'

programme.title: Man Fire Food: Heavy Metal Meat
programme.desc: Roger Mooking gets blown away by not one but two of the biggest metal-clad rigs he's ever seen. In Algoma, WI, he meets brothers Brad and Aric Schmiling who use a giant cinder block pit and massive metal grates to roast a whole steer.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: Crazy Contraptions
programme.desc: Roger Mooking goes from the west coast to east coast to check out crazy contraptions.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Savannah, GA
programme.desc: Casey Webb travels down to Georgia to taste some the Savannah's best fried chicken, low country seafood boil and a four-pound pulled pork sandwich known only as the Pigzilla.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Atlanta, Ga
programme.desc: Casey Webb's cross-country culinary crusade brings him to Atlanta for a decadent doughnut brunch, beastly barbecue and a five-pound mountain of tater tot nachos.

programme.title: Man v. Food Nation: Orange County, CA
programme.desc: Casey Webb cruises into Orange County, California, to take down a three-pound burger slathered in a sauce made from three of the spiciest peppers on the planet.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Palm Springs, CA
programme.desc: Casey Webb's cross-country culinary quest takes him back in time to Palm Springs, California, where he comes face-to-face with a three-pound skyscraper of a sandwich loaded with every deli meat imaginable.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Hall of Fame: Most-Epic Wins
programme.desc: Casey's multi-year campaign against cuisine has resulted in numerous W's and now find out which was the most epic.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Hall of Fame: Sizzling Southwest
programme.desc: Enchiladas, burritos, fajitas, ole! We're counting them down one by one, culminating in a Tex Mex Treat so stupendous, it's coming in at number one on the countdown.

programme.title: Eddie Eats America: San Antonio
programme.desc: Strongman Eddie hits San Antonio to sample the finest Tex-Mex food and a gigantic chicken fried steak.

programme.title: Eddie Eats America: Manhattan
programme.desc: Stoke strongman Eddie Hall goes head to head against long-time rival Brian Shaw, pulling a 23-ton truck and attempting the notorious skyscraper 'Monster' sandwich from Sarge's Deli.

programme.title: Seaside Snacks and Shacks: Endless Summer
programme.desc: Sabin Lomac heads to Calabash, N.C., for a calabash stew with fresh seafood and a one-two punch of sausage and deep-fried corn at Waterfront Seafood Shack.

programme.title: Beach Bites With Katie Lee: Sweet Indulgences

programme.title: Cheap Eats: Providence Eats
programme.desc: Ali starts off his challenge with a Portuguese sweet bread French toast for breakfast, in Providence.

programme.title: Cheap Eats: Chowin' Down In Portland
programme.desc: Food blogger, Ali Khan, has 12 hours and only 35 bucks to find the best deals in Portland.

programme.title: Cheap Eats: Eating Vermont
programme.desc: Ali kicks things off with a local favorite for breakfast in Burlington, Vermont.

programme.title: Cheap Eats: Philly Food Fight

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain: No Reservations: Cuba
programme.desc: In a stadium filled with exuberant fans, Tony experiences baseball through the eyes of the Cuban spectator. Then he visits Robert Salas, who shares some remarkable photographs of Castro and the revolution.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations: Kyoto
programme.desc: Andrew explores the elaborate feasts, home-cooked comfort food, sushi and confections of Kyoto, Japan. From the artistic multi-course dining experience to the everyday humble comfort foods.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations: Savannah
programme.desc: Andrew dives into the flavour-packed favourites of Savannah, Georgia, like creamy shrimp and grits, briny oysters in a half shell, upscale crab bisque and down-home barbecue stew so thick it'll make your spoon stand up.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations: Chesapeake Bay
programme.desc: Andrew tastes his way through the Chesapeake Bay area, trying creamy peanut soup and more.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations: Jerusalem
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern investigates the rich culinary heritage of Jerusalem. From savory classics like shawarma, falafel, kubbeh and hummus to tempting pastries like halva and bureka, this holy city's edible icons are steeped in history.

programme.title: The Best Thing I Ever Ate: Snack Attack
programme.desc: From pickles to tacos, nachos to nuts, we reveal the snackalicious snacks craved by Giada, Ted Allen, Alex Guarnaschelli, Curtis Stone and more.

programme.title: The Best Thing I Ever Ate: Crunchy
programme.desc: Duff, Claire and Brian discuss some of their favourite crispy foods to eat.

programme.title: The Best Thing I Ever Ate: Back to School
programme.desc: We're going back to school to learn once and for all what the ultimate nostalgic dish is. We're joining Chef Beau MacMillan in the cafeteria with his Supreme Boffa super-sub sandwich.

programme.title: The Best Thing I Ever Ate: Trigger Foods
programme.desc: We're losing all control and giving in to ultimate food cravings. Geoffrey Zakarian starts things off like never before with the most juicy and succulent roast chicken from New York City. Jonathon Sawyer takes it to the next level.

programme.title: The Best Thing I Ever Ate: Chocolate Bliss
programme.desc: When it comes to chocolate, the possibilities for sweet treats are endless. On the ultimate list of the best chocolate treats in America, Ali Khan devours an unbelievable Dark Truffle Cake from San Antonio, Texas.

programme.title: The Best Thing I Ever Ate: Asian Sensations
programme.desc: Take your taste buds on a trip to the Far East with a list of America's best Asian dishes. Martha Stewart shares a perfect meaty powerhouse in New York City; Alex Guarnaschelli uncovers a heavy-hitting pork bun in Minneapolis, and more.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and 'Que: Southern, Spicy and Sweet
programme.desc: Bobby Flay makes a surprise guest appearance as Michael visits an over-the-top barbecue joint in New York with a one-of-a-kind brontosaurus beef rib. In Nashville, local tradition is hot chicken, Michael pushes his personal limit.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and 'Que: Whole Hog Heaven
programme.desc: Michael Symon's in Nashville, where a killer new burger joint encourages you to construct your own custom creations. Then, he hits the heart of Cleveland where a newcomer pushes the boundaries of pub food and house-made brews.

programme.desc: After Bingo loses his collar at a pumpkin patch, he, Rolly and Hissy return to find it. When a neighborhood kid can't trick-or-treat because she's lost her costume, Bingo and Rolly go on a mission to track it down.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Speedy as a Cheetah / The Soup Search
programme.desc: When Bob wishes he could run fast, the pugs find cheetahs to learn the secret of their speed. The pugs go on a journey to find the perfect bowl of soup to help Bob feel better.

programme.title: Vampirina: The Critter Fair / Mirror Monster
programme.desc: Vampirina faces challenges as the new kid in town, making friends and attending school.

programme.title: T.O.T.S.: Rock-A-Bye Birdie/ The Fly-Along
programme.desc: Pip and Freddy must impress Captain Beakman when she joins them for a ride-a-long delivery. When Mimi the mockingbird sings K.C.'s lullaby she puts everyone at T.O.T.S. to sleep.

programme.title: T.O.T.S.: The Ring Bear / Bull of Energy
programme.desc: A tenacious penguin and a kind hearted flamingo train to be delivery birds.

programme.title: Bluey: Fairies/ Work/ Bumpy and the Wise Old Wolfhound
programme.desc: Bluey thinks there are Fairies in the backyard; Bluey and Bingo are hired by Dad at the drainpipe factory; Dad and Bluey send Bingo a video.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Design-a-dog/ Ice, Ice Puggy
programme.desc: Bingo and Rolly accidentally chew Bob's childhood toy. Then, when Bobo's icemaker breaks, the pugs go to Antarctica to get him ice.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Hissy's Kitty/ Polly Wants a Pug
programme.desc: When Bob brings home a lost kitty, Bing and Rolly try find her a home. While in Florida, Bingo and Rolly search for an old friend of Bob's.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: The Bird Beard / The Royal Egg Hunt
programme.desc: When Santa and Mrs. Claus have an unusual problem during their summer vacation, the Pugs and Keia help save their trip! The Pugs help find the Queen of England's jeweled egg.

programme.title: T.O.T.S.: Hiccup Hazard/ The Great Robot Race
programme.desc: Pip’s hiccups threaten their delivery so he asks Freddy to do the impossible: scare them out of him. Pip and Freddy have to prove that delivery birds are better than a stork drone.

programme.title: T.O.T.S.: Back to Cool/ Baby Breakdown
programme.desc: Pip and Freddy deliver a baby Penguin to Pip's hometown. When Pip and Freddy break the conveyor belt at T.O.T.S. they are determined to fix it on their own.

programme.title: Doc McStuffins: Rescue Ronda, Ready for Takeoff/ All Washed Up
programme.desc: Doc extracts a twig from a toy helicopter's propellers. When Donny's toy robot malfunctions after playing in the water, Doc explains to him that not all toys can get wet.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Puzzling Pugs/ Rhapsody in Pug
programme.desc: When Bob can't finish his jigsaw puzzle without a picture of a leopard, Bingo and Rolly go searching for one. The pugs unintentionally create chaos when they go to the symphony.

programme.title: Puppy Dog Pals: Puppies and Pandas / Orange You Glad?
programme.desc: Two puppies who love to have fun take on adventures around their neighbourhood.

programme.title: Bluey: The Show / Ticklecrabs / Escape
programme.desc: The adventures of Bluey, a loveable puppy who lives with her parents and little sister.

programme.title: Muppet Babies: Tagalong Polliwog/ Sparkly Star Switcheroo
programme.desc: Kermit's nephew, Robin, comes to visit and everyone is excited to play with him, but Kermit is very protective. When Rizzo is sad about not making a wish on a shooting star, Summer paints a sparkly star to cheer him up.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Add-a-Dad/ Squirrely Puffs
programme.desc: Dad is too busy to spend quality time with Timmy, so he wishes up 'a-lot-a-dads' to hang out with. It's a race between the Squirrely Scouts and the Cream Puffs to the top of Mount Doom.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: One Man Banned/ Frenemy Mine
programme.desc: After Timmy's rejected from the school band, he wishes everyone would love his music. Timmy saves Vicky from a horrible accident.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Scary GodCouple
programme.desc: Foop becomes Vicky's anti-fairy godparent. Vicky and Foop terrorize Timmy and the kids of Dimmsdale. Foop tells Vicky that the reason Timmy has thwarted her all these years is because he has fairy godparents. Now, they want to destroy the fairies.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Hare Raiser/ The Kale Patch Caper
programme.desc: Despite Timmy's strong warning, Chloe wishes the allegedly 'dangerous' class bunny was free. Chloe resorts to stealing when Timmy's Dad buys the only Kale Patch Kid she needs to complete her collection.

programme.title: Movie: Alvin and the Chipmunks: The Squeakquel
programme.desc: Alvin, Simon and Theodore are under their cousin Toby's care. Upon returning to school, they are tasked with winning a music competition whose cash prize will save the school's music department. They find competition in the form of the Chipettes.

programme.title: The Loud House: Out of the Picture/ Room With a Feud
programme.desc: Afraid they'll be forgotten, Lincoln and Clyde want to make a splash in the yearbook and crash group photos. Seeing his sisters fight with their roommates, Lincoln suggests a room shuffle based on a compatibility test.

programme.title: The Loud House: Kick the Bucket List/ Party Down
programme.desc: Lincoln and Clyde's spring break plans are jeopardized when they only have one day to complete their list. Lori throws a sophisticated party, but it backfires when her guests don't like what she's planned.

programme.title: The Casagrandes: Walk Don't Run/ Croaked
programme.desc: In order to buy a new skateboard, Ronnie Anne and Sid start a dog-walking business that quickly gets out of hand. Then, Ronnie, Anne and Sid teach Adelaide about Day of the Dead to help her get over the death of her frog.

programme.title: The Loud House: Pulp Friction/ Pets Peeved

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Pre-Hibernation Week/ Life of Crime
programme.desc: Sandy is going into hibernation; it's alright to 'borrow' things if you return them.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Boyz Crazy
programme.desc: Mabel joins a secret knitting club and is pressured by her new friends to gossip, but when she tells them that Dipper likes Wendy, she inadvertently puts Dipper's life in danger.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: The Love God
programme.desc: Mabel takes matchmaking too far when she steals a love potion from a real Love God.

programme.title: Married at First Sight: Home Alone
programme.desc: For the first time in Married at First Sight history, production shuts down as the Louisiana stay-at-home order is put into place. And after six weeks of the being on lockdown together, some couples move closer to love while others hit a breaking point.

programme.title: Married at First Sight: Unfiltered: Calling It Quits
programme.desc: Jamie Otis gets candid with Olivia, Karen and Henry as they discuss life in quarantine with their new spouses, recommitment day, Brett and Olivia's decision, and a shocking accusation.

programme.title: Married at First Sight: Stranger Spouse
programme.desc: The stay at home orders have been lifted and the couples venture out for a much-needed getaway in the country. While some renew their bonds, others begin to question if they'll want to stay married on Decision Day.

programme.title: Married at First Sight: Top Wedding Moments

programme.title: Married at First Sight: Couples' Cam: Parting Ways
programme.desc: Shawniece and Jephte take some time apart, Danielle and Bobby experience a heartbreaking tragedy, Jamie and Beth get tested for Covid-19, Steph travels to Aruba without AJ, and Anthony goes to war. With a pillow.

programme.title: Life Today With James and Betty Robison
programme.desc: Life Outreach International encourages the support of missions and relief programs around the world.

programme.title: Joyce Meyer: Enjoying Everyday Life
programme.desc: Joyce Meyer, one of the world's leading practical Bible teachers and New York Times best-selling author, shares encouragement and advice to help us enjoy our daily lives.

programme.title: The Balancing Act: Health & Literacy Tips
programme.desc: Strengthening your child's reading skills; what you need to know about psoriasis; vitamins to improve your health; navigating the workplace during the pandemic; contact lenses to help those over 40.

programme.title: The Closer: Dial 'M' for Provenza
programme.desc: Things spiral out of control when Provenza goes undercover to look into a murder for hire.

programme.title: The Closer: Problem Child
programme.desc: The case of a troubled missing boy quickly turns into a homicide investigation.

programme.title: The Closer: Sudden Death
programme.desc: When Sanchez's little brother is shot, the team turns its efforts to finding out if this is related to a past case of the detective's. Meanwhile, a reporter accuses Brenda and her squad of only working on cases that are connected to the rich and powerful.

programme.title: Rizzoli & Isles: Fake It 'til You Make It
programme.desc: After a body turns up in Boston with links to another murder in Los Angeles, Jane and Maura pack up their sunscreen and hop on a plane. With the girls out of town, Angela and Korsak plan a double date with Kiki and Ron.

programme.title: Rizzoli & Isles: 5:26
programme.desc: When an old watch is found inside a balloon of cocaine in a dead woman's stomach, figuring out its significance becomes the key to protecting Jane and the people she loves.

programme.title: Rizzoli & Isles: Hide and Seek
programme.desc: When Maura is abducted, nothing will stand in the way of Jane and the team bringing her home. Far away, Maura discovers a conspiracy that is bigger than just her kidnapping, with Jane as its ultimate target.

programme.title: Rizzoli & Isles: Murderjuana
programme.desc: A security guard foils a robbery when he kills one of the robbers, despite being wounded himself. Jane and Maura work together to solve the case after the search for the robber's accomplice raises some puzzling questions.

programme.title: Rizzoli & Isles: Scared to Death
programme.desc: A woman is found murdered in a grisly scene reminiscent of a horror movie, leading the team to uncover a frightening 'game' in which people pay to experience terror.

programme.title: Rizzoli & Isles: East Meets West
programme.desc: When a gun battle rages in a quiet neighborhood, leaving one dead, Jane and Maura are thrown into the secretive world of an Eastern European gang. Maura tries home-remedies for a fever that have a surprising effect.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Legionnaires' Disease
programme.desc: History was made in Philadelphia in the summer of 1976 -- not because of the bicentennial, but because of the mysterious death of 34 Legionnaires.

programme.title: Forensic Files: The Wilson Murder
programme.desc: An eye doctor is found beaten to death, by his wife. When police arrest an itinerant painter for the murder, he accuses the wife and her sister of hiring him to kill the doctor. Investigators must use forensic science to work out just what happen.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Sworded Scheme
programme.desc: A college student goes missing, and investigators discover blood spatter in her boyfriend’s bedroom. Blood spatter someone has gone to great lengths to conceal.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Unmasked
programme.desc: A serial rapist was on the loose in a Texas town. No one knew where he'd strike next, but the audacity of his attacks and the escalation of violence against his victims made finding him imperative.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Disrobed
programme.desc: A married couple was found shot to death in their suburban home. Investigators thought they had pin-pointed the killer and his motive until trash revealed the shocking truth and the true killer's identity.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Driven to Silence
programme.desc: An attractive young hairstylist is murdered in her South Carolina salon, and police are at a loss. A tiny hair and a DNA test force police to take another look nearly a decade after the crime.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Palm Saturday
programme.desc: 'The Brian and Beverly Mauck Case - an attractive young couple is found dead in their Graham, Washington home and all signs point to a professional hit. But dogged investigators discover some valuable evidence that proves otherwise.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Runaway Love
programme.desc: A woman is found floating in the harbor of an upscale yacht club, stabbed more than 50 times, and her ex-husband and daughter are missing. Investigators turn to forensic science to determine if they’re searching for victims... or perpetrators.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Textbook Murder
programme.desc: A woman is brutally murdered and police point to her fiancé when they learn that he was having affairs with other women. It is not until a man comes forward with information that he is no longer a suspect.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Filtered Out
programme.desc: When a teenage girl goes missing, no one knows if she ran away or if she was the victim of foul play. Everyone's worst fears are confirmed when a body is found at the bottom of an isolated ravine.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Bitter Potion
programme.desc: When a woman comes down with flu-like symptoms and eventually dies, a neurologist suspects she was poisoned with radioactive thallium. The FBI suspects her neighbour, who had a very extensive background in chemistry.

programme.title: Forensic Files: The Blood Trail
programme.desc: In a quiet village in Great Britain, a farmer came upon a chilling sight. Impaled on his fence post was a severed lamb’s head along with a note which read, 'You next.'

programme.title: Forensic Files: The Talking Skull
programme.desc: A mapmaker was surveying the grounds of a boy scout ranch when something caught his eye. At first, it appeared to be a turtle shell, but it proved to be a human skull.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Deadly Delivery
programme.desc: A serial bomber was waging a vendetta against the legal profession: A judge, two attorneys and even a courthouse had been targeted. Investigators used evidence from the bombs to lead them to the perpetrator.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Hair Line
programme.desc: Doctors don't know why the young scientist is gravely ill. When tests finally reveal the cause, it's too late to save him. Police hope that lab analysis of his hair - showing when attempts were made on his life and what was used - will lead to the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files: All That Glitters Is Gold
programme.desc: A bullet-riddled car, a missing driver and no witnesses poses a mystery, until a tiny and unique clue is found.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Deadly Rebellion
programme.desc: Workers discovered a teen's half-naked body on the side of the road, her throat slit. Police hoped the single foreign hair found in a defensive wound on her thumb would lead them to the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Sign of the Crime
programme.desc: Fire rips through a Seattle woman's apartment and some unsettling evidence is found in the rubble. Shortly after, a second fire breaks out just 100 feet from the first, and evidence points to a serial killer whose skills are evolving.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Planted Evidence
programme.desc: A woman is found beaten, bound, strangled and possibly raped outside of Phoenix. The nearby plants tell investigators more about the killer than any other single piece of evidence.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Southside Strangler
programme.desc: Learn how investigators caught Timothy Wilson Spencer, better known as the Southside Strangler, a serial killer responsible for the murders of five women in Virginia. This was the first use of DNA profiling in the U.S. justice system.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Legionnaires' Disease
programme.desc: History was made in Philadelphia in the summer of 1976 -- not because of the bicentennial, but because of the mysterious death of 34 Legionnaires.

programme.title: Forensic Files: The Wilson Murder
programme.desc: An eye doctor is found beaten to death, by his wife. When police arrest an itinerant painter for the murder, he accuses the wife and her sister of hiring him to kill the doctor. Investigators must use forensic science to work out just what happen.

programme.title: How It Really Happened: Did Scott Peterson Murder Laci?
programme.desc: A tale of murder, adultery, courtroom drama, and ultimately the death penalty. The story of Scott and Laci Peterson as told by those who lived it.

programme.title: Morning Express With Robin Meade
programme.desc: A daily dose of news, weather, sports, entertainment, finance and travel, in a fast paced, approachable style.

programme.title: Morning Express With Robin Meade
programme.desc: A daily dose of news, weather, sports, entertainment, finance and travel, in a fast paced, approachable style.

programme.title: Morning Express With Robin Meade

programme.title: Morning Express With Robin Meade
programme.desc: A daily dose of news, weather, sports, entertainment, finance and travel, in a fast paced, approachable style.

programme.title: Lies, Crimes and Video: Chained by a Serial Killer
programme.desc: Prominent realtor Todd Kohlhepp’s double life is exposed when police launch a dramatic rescue – all caught on body camera – of a young woman chained like a dog inside a shipping container on his property.

programme.title: Lies, Crimes and Video: Crazy, Sexy and Deadly
programme.desc: Shayna Hubers and Ryan Poston seemed like a match made in heaven, until he ends up dead in a pool of blood, shot in the face. Stunning interrogation tapes leave cops in disbelief and lead investigators to a surprising motive.

programme.title: Lies, Crimes and Video: Murder in the Church
programme.desc: Missy Bevers was a mom and a fitness instructor in her community. As she’s setting up for an early-morning workout class at the local church, surveillance cameras capture a disturbing series of images; her killer, lurking the halls.

programme.title: Lies, Crimes and Video: Shopping With a Killer
programme.desc: 8-year-old Cherish Perrywinkle is lured out of a Walmart by a registered sex offender, but that’s just the beginning of a night of terror, all caught on camera.

programme.title: Lies, Crimes and Video: Killer Dad: Chris Watts Speaks
programme.desc: ‘Loving’ father Chris Watts turns into one of the nation's most notorious killers overnight. Secret videos document a series of chilling murders from start to finish, revealing lies, a scandalous affair and a shocking coverup.

programme.title: Forensic Files: Payback
programme.desc: In 1993 a 20 year old boy is found beaten to death and murdered in Florida. Soon, a bizarre story unfolds of sex, jealousy, and revenge.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Profiles of intriguing crimes, accidents and outbreaks of disease from around the world are examined.

programme.title: Dodgeball Thunderdome: Hammer Time
programme.desc: A rocket scientist, a social media guru and the Lebron James of Dodgeball compete for one of the final spots in the championship round. Twists and turns on the course abound, including a mighty new weapon that will make even the God of Thunder tremble!

programme.title: Dodgeball Thunderdome: Mother of All Thunderdomes
programme.desc: Exploding balls, a barrage of buckshot and a new adversary making its debut all plague contestants competing for a shot at the $25,000 grand prize. A realtor tries to close the deal and a hospital administrator may need his employee discount.

programme.title: Dodgeball Thunderdome: Joy, Sparkle, and Pain
programme.desc: Only two spots remain in the Tournament of Champions, where the winner will claim Dodgeball supremacy and a $25,000 prize. A ninja dazzles the competition with theatricality and deception. A queen tries to claim her crown.

programme.title: Dodgeball Thunderdome: Balls to the Wall
programme.desc: It's the final round before the final round. A dancing accountant takes on a bouncing boater and a shooter takes her best shot against a man-child with moves.

programme.title: Dodgeball Thunderdome: Tournament of Champions
programme.desc: This one's for all the blue and red balls. The fiercest competitors from season 1 return to the Thunderdome to claim Dodgeball supremacy above all the rest. By the end, a single champion will claim ultimate bragging rights and the $25,000 prize.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown: Lasseter's Gold
programme.desc: Josh Gates sets off deep into the Australian Outback, following the footsteps of legendary explorer Harold Lasseter, who claims to have found a vast field of gold. Josh follows the clues that ultimately lead to a breathtaking conclusion.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown: India's Atlantis
programme.desc: Josh Gates travels to India in search of Dwarka, a mythical kingdom swallowed by the ocean. To find evidence that the golden city is real, Josh dives into the Arabian Sea and visits a dig site that could rewrite history.

programme.title: Dodgeball Thunderdome: Balls to the Wall
programme.desc: It's the final round before the final round. A dancing accountant takes on a bouncing boater and a shooter takes her best shot against a man-child with moves.

programme.title: Dodgeball Thunderdome: Tournament of Champions
programme.desc: This one's for all the blue and red balls. The fiercest competitors from season 1 return to the Thunderdome to claim Dodgeball supremacy above all the rest. By the end, a single champion will claim ultimate bragging rights and the $25,000 prize.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown: Italy's Barbarian Booty
programme.desc: To find the plundered riches of the Roman Empire, Josh Gates must locate the tomb of the Visigoth king Alaric in Italy. The hunt extends from the capital of Rome to the southern city of Cosenza, where the mayor is spearheading an effort to find the tomb.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown: Hunt for the Metal Library
programme.desc: Josh travels through the jungles of Ecuador to explore the watery depths of Tayos Cave for a legendary set of metal plates believed to be created by an advanced ancient civilization.

programme.title: Ten Commandments of the Mafia
programme.desc: In 2007 Italian police discovered a secret Mafia code of conduct. Former Mobsters reveal what those rules are, how they work and what happened when the Mob began to question the 'Ten Commandments of the Mafia'.

programme.title: Ten Commandments of the Mafia
programme.desc: In 2007 Italian police discovered a secret Mafia code of conduct. Former Mobsters reveal what those rules are, how they work and what happened when the Mob began to question the 'Ten Commandments of the Mafia'.

programme.title: Finding Escobar's Millions: Bring In the DEA
programme.desc: Former DEA agents Chris and Jerry track down one of Pablo's top drug traffickers. Exclusive intel sends them racing to dig up a secret airstrip where the kingpin hid his drug money and a safe house where he stashed millions.

programme.title: Finding Escobar's Millions: What's In The Box?
programme.desc: Former DEA agents break open a mysterious concrete box at a secret narco airstrip and dig for millions at a remote safehouse frequented by Pablo Escobar.

programme.title: Finding Escobar's Millions: Family Jewels
programme.desc: Jerry and Chris investigate a property owned by one of Pablo Escobar's cousins and top lieutenant where millions in diamonds and jewels are rumored to be buried.

programme.title: Finding Escobar's Millions: Pablo's Inner Circle
programme.desc: Two former Medellin cartel members provide new intel that sends Chris and Jerry to investigate cocaine labs and secret hideouts in the remote Columbian countryside.

programme.title: Finding Escobar's Millions: The Chemist
programme.desc: A former Escobar chemist claims to know where $30 million in cash and gold is buried. Chris and Ramon investigate a new property with secret tunnels.

programme.title: Finding Escobar's Millions: Endgame
programme.desc: An informant's erratic behavior forces the team to decide between shutting down the operation and investigating one more narco property that could be the big one.

programme.title: Naked and Afraid: Primal Fear
programme.desc: Mountain man Luke and a homeschooling mom and triathlete named Lindsey are dropped into the dry and thorny landscape of Africa's Namibia, surrounded by some of the deadliest apex predators on the continent.

programme.title: Naked and Afraid: Jungle Love
programme.desc: Two survivalists are challenged to survive the Nicaraguan rain forest. Battling freezing nights, severe dehydration and each other, they must work together to survive even though one is a vegetarian and the other is a meat eater.

programme.title: Naked and Afraid: Playing With Fire
programme.desc: Two survivalists are dropped on a deserted Dominica beach. When Dominica claims a casualty, surviving in a barren tropical terrain may prove to be too great a challenge.

programme.title: Naked and Afraid: The Jungle Curse
programme.desc: Two naked survivalists are challenged to survive for 21 days in the Costa Rican rainforest.

programme.title: Naked and Afraid: Breaking Borneo
programme.desc: Puma Cabra and Julie Wright have to survive in Sabah, Borneo for 21 days.

programme.title: Hoarding: Buried Alive: One Is Good, Two Is Better
programme.desc: As a top model on the Hollywood scene, Louise once ran with Tinseltown's elite, but behind her colorful outfits and personality, she still keeps a dark secret: Louise is a compulsive hoarder.

programme.title: Hoarding: Buried Alive: It's My Junk
programme.desc: When Eileen admits a dark secret to her family, the situation quickly escalates beyond hoarding to a matter of life and death. Compulsive hoarder Dale has packed not one, but five houses full of clutter.

programme.title: My Strange Addiction: Rampant Rats/ Extreme Fingernails
programme.desc: Theresa has more than 60 pet hairless rats that are taking over her tiny studio apartment. Meanwhile, 48-year old, Jazz has been growing her fingernails for 22 years. Her longest nail is two feet long, and she calls them her children.

programme.title: My Strange Addiction: Smells Pine Cleaner/ Roadkill Addict
programme.desc: Jillian has been addicted to smelling pine cleaner for two years and can't go more than 15 minutes without a fix. Meanwhile, Scott is obsessed with picking up dead animals from the road.

programme.title: My Strange Addiction: Addicted to Inflatables/ Butt Injection Addict
programme.desc: Mark has been addicted to his 15 inflatable pool toys, eating, sleeping, and bathing with them. Meanwhile, Karmello has been getting black market butt injections for the last three years.

programme.title: My Strange Addiction: Addicted to Coffee Enemas/ Licks Cats
programme.desc: Trina and her husband, Mike, have done nearly 7,000 coffee enemas over the last two and a half years. Meanwhile, Lisa has been addicted to licking her cat and eating clumps of its hair for three years.

programme.title: My Strange Addiction: Eats Dirt/ Extreme Toenails
programme.desc: Kristie has eaten dirt every day for the last 30 years, consuming more than five tons since her addiction began. Meanwhile, Ayanna is addicted to growing her toenails.

programme.title: My Strange Addiction: Drinks Nail Polish/ Ear Digger
programme.desc: Bertha has been drinking up to five bottles of nail polish every day for the past five years. Jamie can't stop digging in her ears.

programme.title: My Strange Addiction: Wears Diapers/ Eats Dryer Sheets
programme.desc: For Riley, there's nothing like the comfort of wearing a warm, wet diaper. She chooses to live as an adult baby to escape the stress of the real world. And Charmissa stuffs her bra with the dryer sheets she chews on for up to 10 hours a day.

programme.title: My Strange Addiction: Eats Cat Food/ Smells Mothballs
programme.desc: Mary consumes over 900 cat treats and several cans of wet food every day. Alicia has been addicted to smelling mothballs for 15 years.

programme.title: My Strange Addiction: Pony Play/ Addicted to Drinking Paint

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Make Womb for Daddy
programme.desc: Find out who scored free sandwiches while shooting at Blimpie; what classic game inspired the 'Guess What' challenge; and what was worse for Q than the pain he endured during his 'childbirth' punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Sneaking Number Twos, Going Number One
programme.desc: Find out why the jokers love filming at the grocery store, how many pencils were purchased for put-pocketing and why Sal's pee punishment was twice as terrible as it looked.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Guilty as Charged
programme.desc: Find out why Q messed with a fishmonger at Fairway instead of a shopper, what nearly caused Sal to lose it during his UFO presentation and how the crew nabbed close ups from inside the mob during Q's punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: No Good Deed
programme.desc: Find out what after-school activity Joe's mom forbid him from doing, why filming nearly halted during the jack asses challenge and what last minute idea made it into Sal and Joe's charity funds punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Stripteased
programme.desc: Find out what prop Q asked to keep from the dental damn challenge, what glazed delight is known to give Joe great power and how a doctor's appointment inspired Murr's striptease punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Indecent Proposal
programme.desc: Find out why Murr had to shave in under three minutes between scenes of That Ain't Me, what the Jokers love most about Writers Blocked, and where Sal ranks his indecent dance instructor punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Turning the Tables
programme.desc: Find out how the Jokers keep their cover while filming at the mall, and Q's favorite VR word. Plus, learn how long Joe was sore after the table-busting punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Lords of the Ring

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: No Child Left Behind
programme.desc: Find out how you make a 'Grandpa Whoopsie' doll, why winter is the perfect season to play pin for the win and where the idea for Q's dirty diaper challenge came from.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Pulling the Rug
programme.desc: Find out where Joe originally wanted to play hide n' seek, what Q's favourite dessert is, and what the crew was most afraid of during Murr's wig-pulling punishment.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Speech Impediment
programme.desc: Go behind the scenes to find out what singer Q is obsessed with, what Sal's secret ingredient to fake vomit is, and why the Jokers in Yonkers had to muffle their laughter.

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Card Against Humanity

programme.title: Impractical Jokers: Inside Jokes: Bull Shiatsu

programme.title: Beat Bobby Flay: Fright Club
programme.desc: There's a thrill of fear in the air when TV personality Jesse Palmer and Food Network's Eddie Jackson step into Bobby Flay's arena. Frank "The Bull" Terzoli and the City of Brotherly Love's John Patterson compete.

programme.title: Beat Bobby Flay: Chocolate Covered Clash
programme.desc: Chef Michael Voltaggio gets help from an extra special guest, celebrated actress Drew Barrymore, to take down Bobby Flay. They bring in fantastic pastry chefs DaVee Harned and Miriam Rieder Taylor, who have a passion for chocolate.

programme.title: Beat Bobby Flay: The Lady Butchers
programme.desc: Meat enthusiast Courtney Rada and Iron Chef Michael Symon source Cleveland chefs Penny Barend and Melissa Khoury to bring Bobby Flay down. These lady butchers are ready to slice and dice the competition.

programme.title: Guy's Grocery Games: Delivery: Budget Games
programme.desc: Guy and Hunter Fieri send inexpensive ingredients to their favourite chefs, who have to make budget-conscious high-end dinners and family meals in their own kitchens. Guy and Hunter play Flavortown's classic budget games to make things more challenging.

programme.title: Beat Bobby Flay: The Nightmare Before Pastry
programme.desc: Katie Lee and Zac Young want to scare up a victory, but Bobby Flay isn't giving up without a fright. Pastry chefs Michael McGowan and Kareem Queeman have a scream at the spooky star ingredient and hope to leave the kitchen with a night of the living win.

programme.title: Beat Bobby Flay: Fright Club
programme.desc: There's a thrill of fear in the air when TV personality Jesse Palmer and Food Network's Eddie Jackson step into Bobby Flay's arena. Frank "The Bull" Terzoli and the City of Brotherly Love's John Patterson compete.

programme.title: Beat Bobby Flay: Chocolate Covered Clash
programme.desc: Chef Michael Voltaggio gets help from an extra special guest, celebrated actress Drew Barrymore, to take down Bobby Flay. They bring in fantastic pastry chefs DaVee Harned and Miriam Rieder Taylor, who have a passion for chocolate.

programme.title: Beat Bobby Flay: The Lady Butchers
programme.desc: Meat enthusiast Courtney Rada and Iron Chef Michael Symon source Cleveland chefs Penny Barend and Melissa Khoury to bring Bobby Flay down. These lady butchers are ready to slice and dice the competition.

programme.title: Guy's Grocery Games: Ghosts of GGG Past
programme.desc: Four GGG competitors are back for redemption, but they might regret it when they face Guy Fieri's most terrifying games yet.

programme.title: Winner Cake All: Guy's Grocery Cakes
programme.desc: Giada De Laurentiis challenges the baking teams to create a cake decoration that looks like their favorite celebratory feast.

programme.title: Restaurant: Impossible: Ambush: Breakfast Break-In
programme.desc: Robert ambushes Jodi, the owner of Theresa's Restaurant in Bradenton, Florida, after her friend and banker, Miriam, puts out a call for help.

programme.title: Restaurant: Impossible: Dull Diner Dilemma
programme.desc: Robert is faced with the problem of making a run-of-the-mill diner stand out in the heart of diner country in North Chelmsford, Massachusetts. After 13 years in business, the co-owners have run out of money and options.

programme.title: Chopped: Hearty Party
programme.desc: The chefs unveil a Cajun specialty in the first basket that is 'offal-y' special. A prized protein adds some porky flavor to the chefs' fish entrees. And a drink that you have to chew is certainly the oddest mystery ingredient in the final basket.

programme.title: Chopped: Something Dumpling
programme.desc: Dumplings are the theme for this competition, as the chefs are instructed to make stuffed morsels of dough inspired by any part of the world. A flavorful pork product and a Japanese condiment make up part of the first mystery basket.

programme.title: Chopped: Smorgastarta Your Engines
programme.desc: The chefs learn all about the wonders of savory Swedish layer cake when they get the competition underway with some smorgastarta appetizers. A sweet powder and prickly produce make the entree round more complicated than the competitors wished.

programme.title: Beat Bobby Flay: Frenemies
programme.desc: Michael Symon and Debi Mazar, two of Bobby Flay's closest friends, stop by for a visit, but their intentions aren't all good.

programme.title: Beat Bobby Flay: Surprise Surprise!
programme.desc: The Windy City's Pat Sheerin and Chinese-American caterer Katie Chin are hoping that what they've got up their sleeves will be enough to send Bobby Flay packing.

programme.title: Beat Bobby Flay: Bobby-Bing Bobby-Boom!
programme.desc: Bobby will have his work cut out for him against two tough Italian chefs and he'll get no sympathy from "Kitchen Cousins" Anthony Carrino and John Colaneri or from Iron Chef Alex Guarnaschelli. It's time to see if Bobby has what it takes to be on top.

programme.title: Beat Bobby Flay: Bobby's DMV Appointment
programme.desc: Cooking Channel's Haylie Duff and Food Network's Curtis Stone aim to knock their friend Bobby Flay down a couple of pegs. Washington D.C. veteran Todd Gray and Maryland's French-trained Gabonese caterer Serge Pambo go head to head.

programme.title: Beat Bobby Flay: Star Power
programme.desc: Food Network Star winners Eddie Jackson and Jeff Mauro have picked who they think is the best to outsmart Bobby Flay in this cooking challenge. Jesse Schenker and Danny Mena step up to the plate to show Bobby who deserves to be called the best.

programme.title: Beat Bobby Flay: America's Funniest Food Show
programme.desc: Things get zany in the kitchen when the king of dad jokes, actor and comedian Bob Saget, teams up with Worst Cooks' Anne Burrell to get the last laugh on Bobby.

programme.title: Beat Bobby Flay: Red Hot
programme.desc: A smokehouse master and a Californian take on Bobby.

programme.title: Beat Bobby Flay: Use Your Noodle
programme.desc: The chips are all in when world-traveled chef Philippe Haddad meets New York's Chef Melissa O'Donnell. It's up to Scott Conant and Geoffrey Zakarian to place their bets on the chef who they think can beat Bobby Flay.

programme.title: Lone Star Law: Midnight Manhunt
programme.desc: A badly injured tortoise needs an innovative procedure and an eagle's death is investigated. Out on the lake, wardens educate the public on staying safe. As night falls, a car crash turns into a manhunt.

programme.title: Lone Star Law: Bucks Collide
programme.desc: Game wardens head to a home after getting a call about bucks fighting in Comal County. While patrolling the Brazos River, Kiel responds to a drowning in progress. A trespassing incident quickly turns into a drug case. Putnam hears shots fired nearby.

programme.title: Lone Star Law: Tempers Are Rising
programme.desc: Warden Cummings deals with an angry man who blew a stop sign. Warden Boone helps chase a trio of armed robbers on the run, and Warden Inman investigates a group of suspected deer hunters for trespassing.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Uncuffed: Panic on the Water
programme.desc: Sgt. Pushee deploys a multi-agency rescue mission to search for a missing kayaker. A pesky bear is playing a game of hide-and-seek in a residential area, and Warden Crouse saves a pair of orphaned raccoon cubs.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Invisible Evidence
programme.desc: Officer Egan investigates the mysterious death of a deer. Officer Courtney and Lt. Cheney help a seal stuck in a tidal creek. Officer Bronson catches a young fisherman in a lie. And Officers lead the search for a woman with a head injury.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Truth Be Told
programme.desc: The wardens encounter all types of people willing to stretch the truth to get out of hot water. Warden Parker searches for a trigger-happy shooter in a family neighborhood. Sergeant Spahr and Warden Lounder investigate a suspiciously shot turkey.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Uncuffed: The Lobsterman Ban
programme.desc: The cuffs are off and the game is on. The Game Wardens of North Wood Law hunt down a notorious lobsterman.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Turkey Dogs
programme.desc: Excited hunters are in the woods because spring turkey hunting season has arrived. Laflamme investigates whether someone may have poached a hen. Spahr responds to a call where a girl was attacked by a dog.

programme.title: North Woods Law: 24 Hours on the Job
programme.desc: Follow several wardens during a busy 24 hours as they patrol for poachers, tag black bears at night, search for a home invader with a K9 team, and respond to a search and rescue.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Running Cold and Fast
programme.desc: Mainers are racing to get their boats out on the water, while the wardens search for an elusive suspect in a jet ski hit-and-run. Also, a mysterious man arrives in Maine just after the Boston Marathon bombings and leaves a suicide note.

programme.title: North Woods Law: Mud Season
programme.desc: The wardens investigate several accidents caused by the mud, including rescuing a boy who was caught in a flash flood. Also, Parker investigates an ATV hit-and-run.

programme.title: Tanked!: Marshawn Lynch Goes Beastmode
programme.desc: Renowned NFL running back and Super Bowl Champion Marshawn Lynch enlists Brett and Wayde to build him the ultimate Beastmode tank.

programme.title: Tanked!: NBA Wizardry
programme.desc: NBA All-Star John Wall asks ATM to build an aquarium shaped like a giant pair of dice. In Georgia they build a huge lighthouse aquarium for a local seafood restaurant that includes engraved dolphins.

programme.title: Tanked!: DJ of Dragons
programme.desc: World famous DJ Steve Aoki wants to get Tanked. But this job will be way harder than pressing play; Steve wants a custom, 3-headed dragon on the verge of escape inside his tank.

programme.title: Tanked!: Going for the Gold(fish)
programme.desc: Wayde and Brett get into the patriotic spirit when Olympic basketball phenom Paul George gives them a call. He wants two identical fish tanks next to his indoor pond, and he wants it done by the time he gets home from the Olympics in Rio.

programme.title: Tanked!: Kevin Smith's Tortally Awesome Tank
programme.desc: Things are about to get fishy for world famous Filmmaker, Kevin Smith. He has two turtles and one tortoise that desperately need a new home. Kevin dreams of a tank that can house both of these species but they require two different living environments.

programme.title: Tanked!: The Wonderful Dr. Oz Tank
programme.desc: Health expert Dr. Oz wants to add to his family. He already has chickens, bees, a cat, and a dog and wants to add some fresh water fish. This aquarium will include an Oz themed interior that will have the fish swimming down the yellow brick road.

programme.title: Tanked!: Clay Matthews' Green Bay Eel Tank
programme.desc: Clay Matthews from the Green Bay Packers is in the process of building his dream home and it wouldn't be complete without a custom tank. Clay's wife wants the interior to stay classy but with subtle references to his Green Bay playing career.

programme.title: Tanked!: Howie Mandel's Surprise Tank
programme.desc: Howie Mandel has reached out to ATM for another tank. Only this time it's a surprise for his manager's company. 3 Arts Entertainment already has an existing tank but it needs a serious upgrade.

programme.title: Tanked!: The Amazing Piano Tank
programme.desc: Pianoworks in Rochester, NY wants Wayde and Brett to build them a piano of their own. This one is made out of acrylic and filled with fish of course.

programme.title: Tanked!: All in the Ocean

programme.desc: New discoveries at the site of an alleged long-lost gold mine could lead investigators to a 200-million-dollar payday, and using cutting-edge tech, experts investigate clues in long-lost maps and relics to recover the Lost Dutchman's treasure.

programme.title: Unexplained and Unexplored: Knights Templar in America
programme.desc: Mysterious new evidence suggests the Knights Templar fled Europe and rebuilt their fearsome warrior order in the Americas a century before Columbus, and experts use cutting-edge tech to investigate if long-lost relics confirm this game-changing theory.

programme.title: Unexplained and Unexplored: Killing Meriwether Lewis
programme.desc: Meriwether Lewis was the man who teamed with William Clark to map the lands west of the Mississippi, but he died in mysterious circumstances. Experts reveal a vast conspiracy that could prove that one of America's greatest explorers was murdered.

programme.title: Unexplained and Unexplored: Mystery of the American Maya
programme.desc: New evidence suggests the Maya civilization did not suddenly vanish, but that they instead still survived, they just resettled in the United States. Using cutting-edge tech, experts reveal what really happened to these long-lost people.

programme.title: Unexplained and Unexplored: Hunt for the Ark of the Covenant
programme.desc: New clues taken from long-lost maps offer fresh leads in the hunt for the Ark of the Covenant, and using the latest hi-tech archaeological methods, experts retrace its steps through the Holy Land, Egypt, and Ethiopia to make a game-changing discovery.

programme.title: Unexplained and Unexplored: Egypt's Land of the Gods
programme.desc: New evidence from one of the world's oldest maps offers clues to a long-lost civilization of untold treasure, and this cutting-edge expedition might finally prove that this mysterious kingdom is the ancestral home of the ancient Egyptians.

programme.title: Unexplained and Unexplored: Finding the Fountain of Youth
programme.desc: The legend of the Fountain of Youth might finally be explained by hidden messages encoded in a 500-year-old map, and using cutting-edge archaeological methods, experts explore forgotten caves and perilous waters to reveal this mystery's long-lost secrets.

programme.title: Ice Cold Killers: Frozen Innocence
programme.desc: On a chilly winter morning in St. Louis, an eleven year old girl vanishes. Days later her body is found along an icy river bank. She's been brutally murdered. It's a race against time for investigators who fear the elusive killer will strike again.

programme.title: Web of Lies: Tainted Love
programme.desc: Retiree Alex Strategos moves to Florida to be closer to his son, but loneliness consumes him. He turns to internet dating and finds 69 year-old Melissa, but is soon struck by a rush of unexplained illnesses.

programme.title: Web of Lies: People You May Know
programme.desc: Single mom Lorianna Parker is excited when she meets popular good-looking Corey Davis online, and filled with hope as their friendship blossoms. But when she goes to meet him in the flesh, her dream date turns into a living nightmare.

programme.title: Web of Lies: The Deadly Hoax
programme.desc: When a tech-savvy computer whiz fails to make the grade as a pro video gamer, he becomes obsessed with finding internet infamy. What starts as an online prank spirals into a new, deadly crime called 'swatting' that chills the nation.

programme.title: Crimes Gone Viral: Watch Dogs
programme.desc: After getting an alert on his computer, a man races home to confront two men burglarizing his home. Then, a woman and her child are defenseless when a strange man enters their home until their 122-pound Great Dane springs into action.

programme.title: Crimes Gone Viral: Who Let the Dogs Out?
programme.desc: It looks like a Hollywood action film as a crazed female driver speeds through neighborhoods in an RV, with two dogs sitting on her lap. And then, a woman tosses a bag of newborn dogs into a dumpster, outraging an entire community.

programme.title: Chaos in Court: Trial by Violence
programme.desc: A father lunges at a serial killer; a defendant attempts to stab an attorney; an Alaskan wildlife enthusiast is tased; a daughter yells at her father's killer; a man threatens a female judge; a defendant walks out of his trial; a man lights up in court.

programme.title: 20/20 on ID: Manhunt
programme.desc: This real life prison break captivated the nation last month and kept a small upstate New York town on edge for three weeks until convicted murderers Richard Matt was shot and killed and David Sweat was shot and captured.

programme.title: Crimes Gone Viral: Watch Dogs
programme.desc: After getting an alert on his computer, a man races home to confront two men burglarizing his home. Then, a woman and her child are defenseless when a strange man enters their home until their 122-pound Great Dane springs into action.

programme.title: Crimes Gone Viral: Who Let the Dogs Out?
programme.desc: It looks like a Hollywood action film as a crazed female driver speeds through neighborhoods in an RV, with two dogs sitting on her lap. And then, a woman tosses a bag of newborn dogs into a dumpster, outraging an entire community.

programme.title: Chaos in Court: Trial by Violence
programme.desc: A father lunges at a serial killer; a defendant attempts to stab an attorney; an Alaskan wildlife enthusiast is tased; a daughter yells at her father's killer; a man threatens a female judge; a defendant walks out of his trial; a man lights up in court.

programme.title: 20/20 on ID: Mystery on the Hudson
programme.desc: A couple goes kayaking on the Hudson River, but only she returns alive. Authorities, piecing together clues, believe his death was deliberate and charge her with murder. But she insists her fiancé's death was simply a tragic accident.

programme.title: Web of Lies: People You May Know
programme.desc: Single mom Lorianna Parker is excited when she meets popular good-looking Corey Davis online, and filled with hope as their friendship blossoms. But when she goes to meet him in the flesh, her dream date turns into a living nightmare.

programme.title: Fatal Vows: Murder Behind the Gates
programme.desc: A couple of opposites come together to form a power couple, but, they soon learn that their money can't buy love and happiness, it can only buy death.

programme.title: Fatal Vows: Everybody Loves Rick
programme.desc: Most high school love ends in heartbreak - but not Richard and Jolena's. Years later, their love is rekindled - but one of them is married to someone else. Tempers flare, trust is betrayed and one in the triangle will end up dead.

programme.title: Forbidden: Dying for Love: Lone Star Lust
programme.desc: A woman enters into an affair with her daughter's grandfather, causing a major rift in the family and eventually leading to a bloody Texas showdown.

programme.title: Forbidden: Dying for Love: Married With Secrets
programme.desc: The dark past of a college student's new husband rears its ugly head and ends in a double homicide.

programme.title: Forbidden: Dying for Love: Laws of Attraction
programme.desc: A high-flying attorney and one of his clients embark on an illicit affair. His infatuation leads to obsession and when their secret affair is revealed, the laws of attraction prove fatal.

programme.title: Forbidden: Dying for Love: Sex and the Married Man
programme.desc: A married man embarks on a forbidden relationship with his 14-year-old babysitter. When his wife finds out, he will stop at nothing to keep his affair secret from the police.

programme.title: Betrayed: Unholy Alliances
programme.desc: Church secretary Dorothy 'Dot' Finelli, 59, goes missing and turns up dead, as police explore if she's killed by a jealous new Internet date or somebody she's known for years within her own family circle.

programme.title: Betrayed: Final Curtain
programme.desc: From devoted mother, to passionate lover to larger-than-life stage actress, forty-year-old Pasinetta Prince plays many roles in her life and she relishes the spotlight. Until a personal betrayal draws the final curtain and ends her life.

programme.title: Betrayed: Murder on the Menu
programme.desc: 17-year-old Mary Jayne Jones is full of nubile innocence when she moves to small town Iowa and gets a job waiting tables. She trusts that there's good in everyone... but she's wrong. And it's a mistake that will cost Jayne her life.

programme.title: Betrayed: The Predator of Pleasant View
programme.desc: Suzy Goulart, 23, is just getting by like everybody else in the scrappy Pleasant View apartment complex, where little dramas between friends and lovers add up to Suzy's fiery murder.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Get Rich Quick
programme.desc: These women think their men are worth more dead than alive. A scorned Southern belle tries to cash in on her ex's life; a countess clings to her riches through violent means; and a mother of two trades her husband for another shot at the good life.

programme.title: Deadly Women: DIY Burial
programme.desc: Dig into the world of these Deadly Women... and in their 'DIY Burial,' you will find blood and bone.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Keep It In the Family
programme.desc: When these Deadly Women strike they keep it 'In The Family'.

programme.title: Hitler: The Actor
programme.desc: Investigates Hitler's breathtaking rise from election defeat in 1928 to Germany's Messiah in 1933. Experts analyze his speeches, dissect his PR gift and interrogate the mind of a man who ruthlessly executes his closest comrade.

programme.title: Top Ten Warfare: Inventions
programme.desc: From the mass production of life-saving Penicillin and pioneering Plastic Surgery, to the development of the Internet and GPS, this episode uses rare archive footage and expert interviews to identify and rank the most influential inventions of warfare.

programme.title: Top Ten Warfare: Leaders
programme.desc: From Winston Churchill and Adolf Hitler, to Mao Tse Tung and Saddam Hussein, experts investigate what makes or breaks a war leader.

programme.title: Chernobyl Disaster: As We Watched
programme.desc: The catastrophic Chernobyl Nuclear Power Plant in the Ukraine created the worst atomic energy accident in world history, endangering the lives of millions of people.

programme.title: Jonestown Massacre: As We Watched
programme.desc: This hour goes inside the People's Temple to see how Jim Jones lured 1000 Americans to Guyana and forced them to die in the largest mass murder-suicide in U.S. history prior to 9/11.

programme.title: Iran Hostage Crisis: As We Watched
programme.desc: In this riveting hour we relive the high stakes drama of the Iran hostage crisis, from the terrifying moment when the American embassy is seized on November 4th, 1979, to the last minute negotiations that freed the hostages 444 days later.

programme.title: The L.A. Riots: 25 Years Later
programme.desc: Revisit the historical tensions and racial injustice that led to the L.A. riots of April 29, 1992. A variety of perspectives and voices are presented, and through modern eyes history is shown to parallel current social issues.

programme.title: Top Ten Warfare: Leaders
programme.desc: From Winston Churchill and Adolf Hitler, to Mao Tse Tung and Saddam Hussein, experts investigate what makes or breaks a war leader.

programme.title: Top Ten Warfare: Inventions
programme.desc: From the mass production of life-saving Penicillin and pioneering Plastic Surgery, to the development of the Internet and GPS, this episode uses rare archive footage and expert interviews to identify and rank the most influential inventions of warfare.

programme.title: Codes and Conspiracies: Secret Prisons
programme.desc: The dark history of America's Secret Prisons is revealed.

programme.title: Codes and Conspiracies: The Vatican
programme.desc: The Vatican is a secretive, watchful institution that protects its reputation and the members of its ranks with fierce, often reckless abandon. The secrets and scandals throughout its history are some of the darkest.

programme.title: Codes and Conspiracies: Civil War
programme.desc: From a secret society that gathers in the dead of night and plots the assassination of Abraham Lincoln, to U.S. spymaster Alan Pinkerton who stops Confederate agents, the outcome of the Civil War is often decided by spies.

programme.title: Project Nazi: The Blueprints of Evil: Retreat from Reality
programme.desc: Adolf Hitler is determined to fight to the very end, even if it costs millions of Germans their lives. He puts his faith in his the 'Wunderwaffe' and the 'wonder weapons,' including the first ever ballistic missile, the V2.

programme.title: Project Nazi: The Blueprints of Evil: Himmler's Empire of Terror
programme.desc: Hitler's bid for power depends on his most loyal, and violent supporters: the SS. Using surveillance, detention, and even murder, they defeat political enemies and are most responsible for putting Hitler's deadly policy of racial purity into practice.

programme.title: Project Nazi: The Blueprints of Evil: The Industry of War
programme.desc: Hitler is determined to rebuild Germany's military strength after the destruction of almost all its weaponry in the First World War. To overcome his country's dire shortage of raw materials, he plans to steal them from other countries.

programme.title: Apocalypse: World War One: Fury
programme.desc: On June 28, 1914, the assassination of an obscure Austrian archduke in Sarajevo triggers the most disastrous conflict the world has ever known. Germany becomes an enemy to France, invades Belgium and, in September, arrives at the gates of Paris.

programme.title: Apocalypse: World War One: Fear
programme.desc: The French Army stops the German advance at the battle of the Marne while the Germans halt the Russians at the Tannenberg on the eastern front.

programme.title: Apocalypse: World War One: Hell
programme.desc: War descends into hell. The battlefield becomes a slaughterhouse. After 16 months of fighting, five million men are dead. Despite this, neither side has gained any ground until a new and terrifying machine rolls onto the battlefield: the tank.

programme.title: Apocalypse: World War One: Rage
programme.desc: People have had enough of the war and uprisings are brewing. German submarine attacks provoke the Americans, but they arrive too late to prevent the Battle of Passchendaele.

programme.title: Apocalypse: World War One: Deliverance
programme.desc: All seems lost for the Allies. The Italians are defeated at Caporette, and a Bolshevik Russia pulls out of the war. But the American reinforcements are decisive, and the Allies emerge victorious.

programme.desc: A couple find a cute 1920s Spanish eclectic home in which they want to update the kitchen, add an ensuite bathroom and dressing room to the master, and plant a drought-tolerant garden, all staying in Spanish style.

programme.title: Restored: 1905 Queen Anne Victorian Restoration
programme.desc: One of Brett's old high school friends asks him to help restore her 1905 Queen Anne Victorian bungalow. She has many unfinished renovation projects but wants to finish them. She also wants to convert a bedroom into the office it was designed to be.

programme.title: Restored: 1940s California Ranch Project
programme.desc: Brett is horrified when a local fire chief and his wife ask him to completely gut and restore their 1940s California Ranch. Brett sets out to show them they can have a kitchen with all the modern conveniences they desire.

programme.title: Restored: 1906 Arts and Crafts House
programme.desc: Brett is called upon to restore a historically significant 1906 arts and crafts home. He brings it back to its original glory by reintroducing the closed-in fireplaces, re-instating the floor plan and replacing the 1980s kitchen.

programme.desc: Preservationist Brett Waterman transforms homes with bad renovations and ugly additions.

programme.desc: Sitting on the shores of Moosehead Lake, the cabin known as the 'Call of The Loon' has been reacquired by the original builder's great-granddaughter. Chase and the team update the amenities and add a second story to accommodate the large family.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: Past, Present and Future
programme.desc: A couple's Belle Island camp has a long multi-generational history within their family spanning nearly 100 years. Their camp is an ongoing focus of family life and they are now ready to make its preservation their main priority.

programme.title: Holmes and Holmes: The River House: Part 1
programme.desc: Mike finds a potential investment for Michael and himself, but the property is three-and-a-half hours away on St. Lawrence River. Things get even trickier as structural issues accumulate, causing stress levels to rise and the budget to go off the rails.

programme.title: Holmes and Holmes: The River House: Part 2
programme.desc: Mike throws Michael a curveball that has major consequences for their business partnership. The rebuild of the River House continues as they battle muck, rain and snow, and after many stressful months, the property is ready to be revealed.

programme.title: Holmes and Holmes: The Suburban House: Part 1
programme.desc: Mike and Michael Jr. take things to the next level as they decide to go into business together. They purchase an outdated, suburban property and face a wonky layout, too many stairs and open-concept headaches.

programme.title: Holmes and Holmes: The Suburban House: Part 2
programme.desc: Mike and Michael figure out the open-concept dilemma at the Suburban House and turn their attention outside, where their plans for a spacious new deck come up short. They must quickly find a solution before the fast-approaching open house arrives.

programme.title: Holmes and Holmes: Holmes Sweet Home
programme.desc: Emotions are high as MJ delivers his custom table to an unsuspecting friend. Then, after months in the making, it's all Holmes on deck as everyone pitches in for the final push to complete the house before the big reveal.

programme.title: Holmes and Holmes: Holmes Sweet Home
programme.desc: Emotions are high as MJ delivers his custom table to an unsuspecting friend. Then, after months in the making, it's all Holmes on deck as everyone pitches in for the final push to complete the house before the big reveal.

programme.title: Treehouse Masters: Camo Treehouse
programme.desc: Pete goes commando for a treehouse build for clients passionate for competition and survivalist games. In a grove of 10 hackberry trees, Pete designs a camouflage structure with a luxurious interior that could pamper any hardened champion.

programme.title: Treehouse Masters: Treehouse 'Z'
programme.desc: Pete connects with old friends in Canton, NC to build them a Zen-like sanctuary in the trees. Battling a record-fast 9-day build window, the team stays calm in constructing a retreat 15-ft high with a picture perfect view of the Blue Ridge Mountains.

programme.title: Treehouse Masters: Nature's Super HQ
programme.desc: Pete and NTS give superhero efforts to build a treehouse for the For-Mar Nature Preserve just outside Flint, MI. The 5-platform 'nature headquarters' will serve as a positive addition to a community still reeling from the water crisis that began in 2014.

programme.title: Treehouse Masters: Home Tree Home
programme.desc: A treehouse escape grows into a full-time live-in treehouse when Pete lays eyes on the gargantuan Sitka spruce of his dreams for a hexagonal build on the coast of Oregon.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and 'Que: Finger-Licking Good
programme.desc: Alex Guarnaschelli joins Michael Symon at one of his favorite Manhattan spots, where they combine a bottomless beer selection with the most high-octane hot dog known to man.

programme.title: Burgers, Brew and 'Que: Sweet and Smokey
programme.desc: Michael Symon starts in his hometown of Cleveland, where a place he went as a kid combines several classic sweets to make a one-of-a-kind dessert burger.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Meat and Mandarins
programme.desc: Noah gets hot for the smokin' okra burger at the Eastern Carolina Agricultural Fair in Florence, SC. Then he launches into atomic habanero tots and 'The Big Yum' at the Mountain Mandarin Festival in Auburn, CA.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Royals and the Red River City
programme.desc: Noah begins his journey at the Royal Winter Fair in Toronto, Canada, where he says "yes, cheese" to the blue balsamic. Later, he ends his culinary adventure at the State Fair of Louisiana with the sweet Not Yo' Average Nachos.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Lights and Literature
programme.desc: Noah tries his hand at making a deep fried root beer float and torches a berry brulee waffle.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Burgers and Bombs
programme.desc: Start the season off right with whacky takes on some fairground classics, with The Waffle Burger and the Donut Philly Cheesesteak. 'Zanado Fries and a S'mores Cobbler round off the day.

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Super Sundaes
programme.desc: We all scream for ice cream, but when it comes to super sundaes, these carnival concessionaires have gone one step beyond, and host Noah Cappe is here to try them all!

programme.title: Carnival Eats: Pretzella Queen of the Dessert
programme.desc: Host Noah Cappe enjoys German inspired carnival treats at Christkindlmarket Milwaukee. First Noah digs into the Austrian Alpine Daug which packs a cheese-stuffed bratwurst into a hearty roll topped with sauerkraut and potato salad.

programme.title: Food Paradise: Neighborhood Hangouts
programme.desc: From favourite friendly diners to special occasion restaurants, Food Paradise visits some of the coolest neighborhood hangouts that prove this local food is all good in the 'hood.

programme.title: Food Paradise: Extreme Eats
programme.desc: Food Paradise is going to extremes with the best in big food with even bigger flavour. Whether it's a Bloody Mary garnished with baby back ribs, mozzarella sticks and more or a palatial pizza made of chicken Parmesan, sometimes too much is not enough!

programme.title: Food Paradise: Food Hall of Fame
programme.desc: New dining experiences and classic comforts are brought together under one roof at these food halls, from spicy fried chicken paired with decadent mac and cheese at Atlanta's Krog Street Market to a pierogi melt at Cleveland's West Side Market.
